label: Traps
title: Traps
bodyHtml:
Traps are advanced buildings

Traps are still just BuildingEntity objects registered through CustomBuildingEntityDefinition and a custom script. Please read the BuildingEntity documentation for more details before this page, as this is only meant for a practical example.

What to keep in mind

Use Placement = BuildingPlacementType.Floor for floor traps, keep RandomFlip off if the sprite cannot flip horizontally without readability issues, and use a small SurfaceOffset so the trap sits naturally on the ground.

If your trap has a delayed trigger, make the pre-trigger state obvious. A light, sound, or animation helps players understand that the object is armed/active instead of bugged.

After the effect finishes, disable collision and visuals or destroy the object so it does not keep re-triggering. If the trap needs persistent state, move that state into a save provider instead of relying on the scene object alone.

Spore Mine

The example on the right is of a spore mine trap. Notice the SporeMineScript component that manages the trigger radius, detonation delay, warning light, and gas cloud.
